assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states PDBs are the most common cause and prioritizes them as Step 1, saying '90% of stuck drains'

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' and 'kubectl describe pdb PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE' commands

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: bare pods check with jq, pending pods check, events filtering for EvictionBlocked and Killing reasons

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Section 3 specifically covers resource constraints with commands to check pending pods, node resources, and failed scheduling events

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Shows kubectl patch command to temporarily adjust PDB maxUnavailable to 50%

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE's PDB timeout behavior or automatic force-drain after timeout

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Section 3 shows gcloud command to adjust max-surge-upgrade and max-unavailable-upgrade settings

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Response does not mention admission webhooks or webhook issues as a potential cause of stuck drains

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Provides monitoring commands with 'watch' to track node transitions and pod movements, plus post-fix validation steps

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE's specific disruption-event notifications, POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub integration
